task_completion.description: The agent opens the edit form or settings for the 'Athlete Diet' nutrition plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A modal, form, or page titled 'Edit nutrition plan' (or similar) is visible, specifically for the 'Athlete Diet'.

task_completion.description: The agent locates the input field for the daily energy goal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An input field labeled 'Energy', 'Calories', 'kcal', or 'Daily goal' is visible within the edit form.

task_completion.description: The agent enters exactly 2500 into the energy goal field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value '2500' is visible inside the energy/kcal input field.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the updated nutrition plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a 'Save', 'Submit', or 'Update' button at the bottom of the edit form.

task_completion.description: The 'Athlete Diet' nutrition plan displays the updated daily energy goal of 2500 kcal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The nutrition plan overview page for 'Athlete Diet' shows '2500' (or '2500 kcal') in the energy goal section or summary.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the wger web interface via the browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser window showing the wger application is visible, with no terminal or API testing tools used to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The agent edited the existing 'Athlete Diet' plan rather than creating a new one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen shows the agent editing the already open 'Athlete Diet' plan, not clicking 'Add nutrition plan'.

integrity.description: The final state reflects genuine application interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final 2500 kcal value is displayed within the standard wger UI layout, without signs of browser developer tools (Inspect Element) being used to fake the text.
